content_en:
“Umar added, "I met Abu Bakr As-Siddeeq and said to him, ‘If you wish, I will give my daughter Hafsah in marriage to you." Abu Bakr kept silent and did not say any word to me in reply. | got more furious with him than with ~Uthmaan. I waited for a few days, and then Allah's Messenger asked for her hand, and I gave her in marriage to him. Afterwards I met Abu Bakr who said to me, ‘Perhaps you got angry with me when you offered Hafsah in marriage to me, and I did not give you a reply?' I said, 'Yes.' Abu Bakr said, 'Nothing prevented me to respond to your proposal except that I knew that Allah's Messenger had mentioned her, and I never wanted to let out the secret of Allah's Messenger. And if Allah's Messenger had refused her, I would have accepted her.' " (Reported by AlAl-Bukhari.)

Abu Bakr's Tender Heart:

On the authority of *Aa'ishah, may Allah be pleased with her, who says: “I had seen my parents practicing Islam since I attained the age of puberty. Not a day passed but the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, visited us, both in the morning and the evening. My father Abu Bakr thought of building a mosque in the courtyard of his house, and he did so. He used to pray and recite the Quran in it. The polytheist women and their children used to gather around him and look at him astonishingly. Abu Bakr was a softhearted man and could not help weeping while reciting the Quran. The chiefs of the Quraysh disbelievers became afraid of that (ie. that their

children and women might be affected by the recitation of Quran)." (Reported by Al-Bukhari.)

Anas, may Allah be pleased with him, reports that after the death of Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, Abu Bakr said to ~Umar, "Let us visit Umm Ayman as Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, used to visit her." When they went to her, she wept. They (Abu Bakr and ~Umar) said to her, "What makes you weep? What is in store (in the next world) for Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, is better than (this worldly life)." She said, "I weep not because J am ignorant of the fact that what is in store for Allah's Messenger (in the next world) is better than (this worldly life), but I weep because the Divine Revelation has ceased to come from Heaven. This moved both of them to tears and they began to weep along with her." (Reported by Moslem.)

Abu Bakr's Interpretation of Dreams:

Abu Bakr, may Allah be pleased with him, was an acknowledged interpreter of dreams. The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, himself used to consult only him for interpreting his dreams.

Sa*eed Ibn Mansoor narrates on the authority of Sa* eed Ibn Al-Musayyib who said: "* Aa'ishah, may Allah be pleased with her, saw in a dream that three moons fell in her house. So she narrated her dream to Abu Bakr - who was the best interpreter of dreams - he said: "If you have truly seen that, then three of the best of all Allah's creation will be buried in your house." When the Prophet , peace and blessings be upon him, died, Abu Bakr said to ~Aa'ishah, "This is the best of your three moons.”

Saeed Ibn Mansoor also narrates on the authority of “Umar Ibn Sharahabeel who said: "The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said: "I saw that I was followed by black sheep then I was followed by white sheep that outnumbered the black ones in a way that you could hardly see the black ones. Abu Bakr interpreted the dreams saying: "O Prophet of Allah, the black sheep stand for the Arabs who will embrace Islam in large numbers, and the white sheep stand for the non-Arabs who will embrace Islam in far larger numbers than the Arabs. The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said, "That was exactly how the angel interpreted it before dawn."

Ibn Sa*eed further narrates on the authority of Ibn Shihaab who said: The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, saw a dream and narrated it to Abu Bakr. He said: "I saw in a dream that we were racing and I was ahead of you by steps. “Abu Bakr said, "You will die and rest in peace in Heaven, and I will live two years and a half after your passing away.

On the authority of Abu Qilaabah who says: A man said to Abu Bakr As-Sideeq, "I saw in a dream that I urinate blood," Whereupon Abu Bakr said, "You are a man who copulates with his wife in the time of her menses, so seek Allah's Forgiveness and do not do this again."

Verses, Hadiths and Scholars Saying Referring to Abu Bak's Caliphate:

On the authority of Hudhayfah, may Allah be pleased with him, who quotes the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, as saying: "Among those who will succeed me, follow Abu Bakr and *Umar." (Reported by Al-Tirmidhi and Al-

Jubayr Ibn Mut'am, may Allah be pleased with him, reports: "A woman came to the Prophet, peace and blessing be upon him, to ask him about something, but he told her to come some other time. She said, "O Messenger of Allah! What if I come but do not find you? [t seemed as if she meant that he might die?" The Prophet said. "If you do not find me, then go to Abu Bakr." (Reported by AlBukhari and Moslem.)

Anas, may Allah be pleased with him, narrates: "Banu Al-Mustaliq sent me to the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, in order to ask him to who would they pay alms after the Prophet's demise. I went to the Prophet to ask him about this, and he said to me, "To Abu Bakr." (Reported by AlHaakim.)

*Aa'ishah, may Allah be pleased with her, reports that Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, said to her in his (last) illness.’ "Call Abu Bakr, your father and your brother too, so that I may write a document, for he fear lest someone else might be desirous (of succeeding me) and (that some claimant might say, ‘I have better claim to it, whereas Allah and_ the believers do not substantiate the claim of anyone but that of Abu Bakr. "(Reported by Moslem.)

Ibn Abu Mulaykah reports that * Aa'ishah, may Allah be pleased with her, was asked about the person Allan's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, would have nominated his successor if he had to nominate one at all. She said, "Abu Bakr.” It was said to her, "Then who after Abu Bakr?" "Umar,' she replied. It was said to her, ‘Then who after “Urnar?" She said, ~Abu *Ubaydah Ibn Al-Jarraah, and then she kept silent, (Reported by Moslem.)

Abu Moosa Al-Ash*aryy, may Allah be pleased with him, narrates: "The Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, fell ill and when his illness aggravated, he said, "Tell Abu Bakr to lead the people in Salaah." ~ Aa'ishah said, "O Messenger of Allah, "Abu Bakr is a softhearted man, he would not be able to lead the Salaah in your place." The Prophet said again, "Tell Abu Bakr to lead the people in Salaah." She repeated the same reply, and the Prophet said: "Tell Abu Bakr to lead the people in Salaah. You are even as the women who were with Prophet Yoosuf (Joseph)." So the Messenger of Allah went to Abu Bakr (with that order), and he led the people in Salaah in the lifetime of the Prophet. (Reported by Al-Bukhari and Moslem.)

Scholars have deduced the reference to Abu Bakr's caliphate from some verses of the Quran. Al-Hasan Al-Basri, commenting on the verse:

see DSU sds td pe Ug pb sae oF (So Bg AIST all wiv (0 € 85) eohe Realy AUN eldS yo as'y

"O ye who believe if any from among you turn back from his Faith, soon will Allah produce a people whom He will love as they will love Him, lowly with the Believers, mighty against the rejecters, fighting in the way of Allah, and never afraid of the reproaches of such as find fault. That is the Grace of Allah, which he will bestow on whom he pleaseth. And Allah encompasseth all, and he knoweth all things." (Al-Maa' idah: 54), he said: "By Allah, they arc Abu Bakr and his companions. When some Arabs relinquished Islam after the death of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, Abu Bakr and his Companions fought them until hey returned back to Islam.

Abu Bakr Elected Caliph:

It_ is reported that “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, may Allah be pleased with him, addressed the people, upon his return from Hajj, saying: “I have heard that someone said, 'If ~ Umar were dead, 1 would pledge allegiance to such-and-such person.’ No one should deceive himself by saying that the election of Abu Bakr was an unpremeditated affair (Faltah) which was ratified. Admittedly it was so; However, Allah averted any evil which might have resulted from it. There is none amongst you to whom people would devote themselves as they did to Abu Bakr who was the best of us.

It happened that when Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, passed away, ~ Ali and Az-Zubayr and their Companions withdrew from us in the house of Faatimah, and the Ansaar opposed us and assembled with their chiefs in the hall (Saqeefah) of Banu Saa*idah; while the Muhajireen gathered around Abu Bakr. ~Umar continued: "I told Abu Bakr that we should go to our brothers, the Ansaar, when two righteous men met us and told us the conclusion the people had come to. They asked us, "Where are you going, O people of the Muhajireen?" 1 replied, ‘To our brothers, the Ansaar.' They told us that there was no need of approaching them, and that we must make our own decision. I said, 'By Allah, we will go to them: we found them in the hall of Banu Saa*idah. In the middle of the hall was aman wrapped up ina cloak. I asked who that man was, and the people answered that he was Sad Ibn ~Ubadah. I wondered, ‘What's wrong with him?’ 'He is ill," came the answer. When we sat down, their orator stood up to address the assembly. He praised and thanked Allah as He deserves, and then said: "We are the Helpers (Ansaar) of Allah and the battalion of Islam; and you, O Muhajireen are a family

of ours and a group of your people have settled amongst us. You are trying to cut us off from our origin and wrest authority from us." When _ he finished, 1 wanted to speak, because I had prepared a speech which pleased me much, and wanted to deliver it before Abu Bakr. I was trying to soften a certain asperity of his, but he (Abu Bakr) was more forbearing and wiser than I was. He said, 'Gently, ~Umar!' I did not like to anger him so | allowed him to speak. He is a man with more knowledge than [, and by Allah he did not omit a single word which T had thought of, and he uttered it in his inimitable way better than I could have done. The orator said: "Truly, you deserve all the good you have said about yourselves, but the Arabs will never accept the authority of anyone other than a man of this clan of Quraysh; they being the best of the Arabs as regards descent and country. I offer you one of these two men. Pledge allegiance to whichever of these you please." Thus saying he took hold of my hand and that of Abu ~ Ubaydah Ibn Al-Jarraah who was silting amongst us. Nothing he said displeased me more than that (proposal). By Allah, I would rather have come forward and have my neck chopped off - if that were no sin — than become the ruler of a people of whom Abu Bakr was one.
content_id:
"Umar menambahkan, "Aku menemui Abu Bakr As-Siddiq dan berkata kepadanya, 'Jika engkau mau, aku akan menikahkan putriku Hafsah denganmu.' Abu Bakr terdiam dan tidak menjawab sepatah kata pun kepadaku. Aku menjadi lebih marah kepadanya daripada kepada Utsman. Aku menunggu beberapa hari, kemudian Rasulullah melamarnya, dan aku menikahkannya dengan beliau. Setelah itu aku bertemu Abu Bakr yang berkata kepadaku, 'Mungkin engkau marah kepadaku ketika engkau menawarkan Hafsah untuk menikah denganku, dan aku tidak memberimu jawaban?' Aku berkata, 'Ya.' Abu Bakr berkata, 'Tidak ada yang menghalangiku untuk merespons lamaranmu kecuali bahwa aku tahu Rasulullah telah menyebutkan tentangnya, dan aku tidak pernah ingin membocorkan rahasia Rasulullah. Seandainya Rasulullah menolaknya, niscaya aku akan menerimanya.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)

Kelembutan Hati Abu Bakr:

Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah, semoga Allah meridhainya, yang berkata: "Aku telah melihat kedua orang tuaku menjalankan Islam sejak aku mencapai usia baligh. Tidak sehari pun berlalu tanpa Nabi, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, mengunjungi kami, baik di pagi maupun sore hari. Ayahku Abu Bakr berniat membangun masjid di halaman rumahnya, dan beliau melakukannya. Beliau biasa salah dan membaca Quran di sana. Para wanita musyrik dan anak-anak mereka berkumpul di sekelilingnya dan memandangnya dengan takjub. Abu Bakr adalah seorang yang berhati lembut dan tidak kuasa menahan tangis saat membaca Quran. Para pemimpin kafir Quraisy menjadi khawatir akan hal itu (yaitu bahwa anak-anak

dan wanita mereka mungkin terpengaruh oleh bacaan Quran)." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)

Anas, semoga Allah meridhainya, meriwayatkan bahwa setelah wafatnya Rasulullah, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, Abu Bakr berkata kepada Umar, "Mari kita mengunjungi Ummu Ayman sebagaimana Rasulullah, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, biasa mengunjunginya." Ketika mereka mendatanginya, ia menangis. Mereka (Abu Bakr dan Umar) berkata kepadanya, "Apa yang membuatmu menangis? Apa yang tersedia (di akhirat) bagi Rasulullah, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, lebih baik daripada (kehidupan dunia ini)." Ia berkata, "Aku menangis bukan karena aku tidak tahu bahwa apa yang tersedia bagi Rasulullah (di akhirat) lebih baik daripada (kehidupan dunia ini), tetapi aku menangis karena wahyu Ilahi telah berhenti turun dari langit." Hal ini mengharukan keduanya hingga menangis dan mereka pun ikut menangis bersamanya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim.)

Kemampuan Abu Bakr dalam Menafsirkan Mimpi:

Abu Bakr, semoga Allah meridhainya, diakui sebagai penafsir mimpi yang handal. Nabi, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, sendiri hanya berkonsultasi kepadanya untuk menafsirkan mimpi beliau.

Sa'id bin Mansur meriwayatkan dari Sa'id bin Al-Musayyib yang berkata: "Aisyah, semoga Allah meridhainya, bermimpi tiga bulan jatuh di rumahnya. Maka ia menceritakan mimpinya kepada Abu Bakr — yang merupakan penafsir mimpi terbaik — beliau berkata: 'Jika benar engkau melihat itu, maka tiga orang terbaik dari seluruh ciptaan Allah akan dimakamkan di rumahmu.' Ketika Nabi, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, wafat, Abu Bakr berkata kepada Aisyah, 'Inilah bulan terbaik dari tiga bulanmu.'"

Sa'id bin Mansur juga meriwayatkan dari Umar bin Syarahbil yang berkata: "Nabi, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: 'Aku bermimpi diikuti oleh domba-domba hitam, kemudian aku diikuti oleh domba-domba putih yang jumlahnya melebihi domba hitam sehingga kamu hampir tidak bisa melihat domba hitamnya.' Abu Bakr menafsirkan mimpi itu dengan berkata: 'Wahai Nabi Allah, domba hitam melambangkan orang-orang Arab yang akan memeluk Islam dalam jumlah besar, dan domba putih melambangkan orang-orang non-Arab yang akan memeluk Islam dalam jumlah yang jauh lebih besar daripada orang Arab.' Nabi, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, bersabda, 'Begitulah persis malaikat menafsirkannya sebelum fajar.'"

Ibnu Sa'id selanjutnya meriwayatkan dari Ibnu Syihab yang berkata: Nabi, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, bermimpi dan menceritakannya kepada Abu Bakr. Beliau bersabda: "Aku bermimpi bahwa kita sedang berlomba dan aku mendahuluimu beberapa langkah." Abu Bakr berkata, "Engkau akan wafat dan beristirahat dengan tenang di surga, dan aku akan hidup dua tahun setengah setelah kepergianmu."

Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qilabah yang berkata: Seorang laki-laki berkata kepada Abu Bakr As-Siddiq, "Aku bermimpi kencing berdarah." Abu Bakr menjawab, "Engkau adalah seorang laki-laki yang menggauli istrinya saat haid, maka mohonlah ampunan Allah dan jangan ulangi lagi."

Ayat-ayat, Hadith dan Perkataan Ulama yang Merujuk pada Kekhalifahan Abu Bakr:

Diriwayatkan dari Hudzaifah, semoga Allah meridhainya, yang mengutip Nabi, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, bersabda: "Di antara orang-orang yang akan menggantikanku, ikutilah Abu Bakr dan Umar." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Tirmidzi dan Al-

Jubair bin Mut'im, semoga Allah meridhainya, meriwayatkan: "Seorang wanita datang kepada Nabi, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, untuk menanyakan sesuatu, tetapi beliau menyuruhnya datang lain waktu. Ia berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah! Bagaimana jika aku datang tetapi tidak menemukanmu? Sepertinya ia bermaksud bahwa beliau mungkin wafat.' Nabi bersabda, 'Jika kamu tidak menemukanku, maka pergilah kepada Abu Bakr.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim.)

Anas, semoga Allah meridhainya, meriwayatkan: "Banu Al-Mustaliq mengutusku kepada Nabi, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, untuk menanyakan kepada siapa mereka harus membayar zakat setelah wafatnya Nabi. Aku mendatangi Nabi untuk menanyakan hal ini, dan beliau bersabda kepadaku, 'Kepada Abu Bakr.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Hakim.)

Aisyah, semoga Allah meridhainya, meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, bersabda kepadanya dalam sakitnya (yang terakhir): "Panggillah Abu Bakr, ayahmu, dan juga saudaramu, agar aku menulis sebuah dokumen, karena aku khawatir seseorang mungkin berkeinginan (untuk menggantikanku) dan (seseorang yang mengklaim mungkin berkata, 'Akulah yang lebih berhak atasnya,' padahal Allah dan kaum mukminin tidak membenarkan klaim siapa pun kecuali Abu Bakr." (Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim.)

Ibnu Abu Mulaykah meriwayatkan bahwa Aisyah, semoga Allah meridhainya, ditanya tentang siapa yang akan Rasulullah, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, tunjuk sebagai penggantinya jika beliau harus menunjuk seseorang. Ia menjawab, "Abu Bakr." Ditanyakan kepadanya, "Lalu siapa setelah Abu Bakr?" "Umar," jawabnya. Ditanyakan kepadanya, "Lalu siapa setelah Umar?" Ia menjawab, "Abu Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah," lalu ia terdiam. (Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim.)

Abu Musa Al-Asy'ari, semoga Allah meridhainya, meriwayatkan: "Nabi, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, jatuh sakit dan ketika sakitnya bertambah parah, beliau bersabda, 'Perintahkan Abu Bakr untuk mengimami salah.' Aisyah berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, Abu Bakr adalah seorang yang berhati lembut, ia tidak akan mampu mengimami salah menggantikanmu.' Nabi bersabda lagi, 'Perintahkan Abu Bakr untuk mengimami salah.' Ia mengulangi jawaban yang sama, dan Nabi bersabda, 'Perintahkan Abu Bakr untuk mengimami salah. Kalian seperti para wanita yang bersama Nabi Yusuf.' Maka Rasulullah pergi menemui Abu Bakr (dengan perintah itu), dan beliau mengimami salah semasa hidup Nabi." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari dan Muslim.)

Para ulama telah menyimpulkan rujukan kepada kekhalifahan Abu Bakr dari beberapa ayat Quran. Al-Hasan Al-Basri, mengomentari ayat:

see DSU sds td pe Ug pb sae oF (So Bg AIST all wiv (0 € 85) eohe Realy AUN eldS yo as'y

"Wahai orang-orang yang beriman, barangsiapa di antara kamu yang murtad dari agamanya, maka kelak Allah akan mendatangkan suatu kaum yang Allah mencintai mereka dan mereka pun mencintai-Nya, yang bersikap lemah lembut terhadap orang-orang mukmin, tetapi bersikap tegas terhadap orang-orang kafir, yang berjihad di jalan Allah, dan yang tidak takut kepada celaan orang yang suka mencela. Itulah karunia Allah, diberikan-Nya kepada siapa yang dikehendaki-Nya, dan Allah Mahaluas pemberian-Nya lagi Maha Mengetahui." (Al-Maa'idah: 54), beliau berkata: "Demi Allah, mereka adalah Abu Bakr dan para sahabatnya. Ketika sebagian orang Arab meninggalkan Islam setelah wafatnya Nabi, shallallahu alaihi wa sallam, Abu Bakr dan para sahabatnya memerangi mereka hingga mereka kembali kepada Islam."

Abu Bakr Terpilih sebagai Khalifah:

Diriwayatkan bahwa Umar bin Al-Khaththab, semoga Allah meridhainya, berpidato kepada orang-orang, sepulangnya dari haji, dengan berkata: "Aku mendengar seseorang berkata, 'Jika Umar meninggal, aku akan membaiat fulan.' Janganlah seseorang menipu dirinya sendiri dengan mengatakan bahwa pemilihan Abu Bakr adalah urusan yang tidak direncanakan (Faltah) yang kemudian disahkan. Memang benar demikian adanya; namun Allah menghindarkan segala keburukan yang mungkin timbul darinya. Tidak ada seorang pun di antara kalian yang orang-orang mencurahkan kesetiaan kepadanya sebagaimana mereka mencurahkannya kepada Abu Bakr yang merupakan orang terbaik di antara kita.

Terjadi bahwa ketika Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam wafat, Ali dan Az-Zubayr beserta para sahabat mereka menyingkir dari kami di rumah Fatimah, dan kaum Anshar menentang kami serta berkumpul bersama para pemimpin mereka di balai (Saqifah) Banu Sa'idah; sementara kaum Muhajirin berkumpul di sekitar Abu Bakr. Umar melanjutkan: "Aku berkata kepada Abu Bakr bahwa kita harus pergi menemui saudara-saudara kita, kaum Anshar, ketika dua orang saleh menemui kami dan memberitahu kami kesimpulan yang telah dicapai oleh orang-orang. Mereka bertanya kepada kami, 'Hendak ke mana kalian, wahai kaum Muhajirin?' Aku menjawab, 'Menemui saudara-saudara kami, kaum Anshar.' Mereka memberitahu kami bahwa tidak perlu mendatangi mereka, dan bahwa kami harus membuat keputusan sendiri. Aku berkata, 'Demi Allah, kami akan mendatangi mereka.' Kami menemukan mereka di balai Banu Sa'idah. Di tengah balai itu ada seorang lelaki yang berselimut jubah. Aku bertanya siapa lelaki itu, dan orang-orang menjawab bahwa dia adalah Sa'd bin Ubadah. Aku bertanya-tanya, 'Ada apa dengannya?' 'Dia sedang sakit,' jawab mereka. Ketika kami duduk, juru bicara mereka berdiri untuk berpidato di hadapan majelis. Ia memuji dan bersyukur kepada Allah sebagaimana yang layak bagi-Nya, kemudian berkata: 'Kami adalah para Penolong (Anshar) Allah dan pasukan Islam; dan kalian, wahai kaum Muhajirin, adalah bagian dari keluarga

kami, dan sekelompok dari kaum kalian telah menetap di antara kami. Kalian berusaha memisahkan kami dari asal-usul kami dan merebut kekuasaan dari kami.' Ketika dia selesai, aku ingin berbicara, karena aku telah menyiapkan pidato yang sangat kusukai, dan ingin menyampaikannya sebelum Abu Bakr. Aku berusaha melunakkan kekerasan tertentu darinya, tetapi dia (Abu Bakr) lebih penyabar dan lebih bijaksana dariku. Dia berkata, 'Pelan-pelan, Umar!' Aku tidak ingin membuatnya marah, maka aku membiarkannya berbicara. Dia adalah orang yang lebih berilmu dariku, dan demi Allah, dia tidak melewatkan satu kata pun yang telah kupikirkan, dan dia mengucapkannya dengan caranya yang tiada tara, lebih baik dari yang bisa kulakukan. Juru bicara itu berkata: 'Sungguh, kalian layak mendapat semua kebaikan yang telah kalian katakan tentang diri kalian, tetapi bangsa Arab tidak akan pernah menerima kekuasaan dari siapa pun selain dari suku Quraysh ini; mereka adalah yang terbaik di antara bangsa Arab dalam hal keturunan dan negeri. Aku menawarkan kepada kalian salah satu dari dua orang ini. Berbaiatlah kepada siapa pun di antara keduanya yang kalian kehendaki.' Seraya berkata demikian, dia menggenggam tanganku dan tangan Abu Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah yang duduk di antara kami. Tidak ada yang dikatakannya yang lebih tidak kusukai selain usulan itu. Demi Allah, aku lebih suka maju ke depan dan leherku dipenggal — jika itu bukan dosa — daripada menjadi pemimpin suatu kaum yang di antaranya terdapat Abu Bakr.
content_ar: “عمر added, "I met أبو بكر As-Siddeeq and said to him, ‘إذا you wish, I will give my daughter Hafsah in marriage to you." أبو بكر kept silent and did not say any word to me in reply. | got more furious with him than with ~Uthmaan. I waited for a few days, and then الله's Messenger asked for her hand, and I gave her in marriage to him. Afterwards I met أبو بكر who said to me, ‘Perhaps you got angry with me عندما you offered Hafsah in marriage to me, and I did not give you a reply?' I said, 'Yes.' أبو بكر said, 'Nothing prevented me to respond to your proposal except that I knew that الله's Messenger had mentioned her, and I never wanted to let out the secret of الله's Messenger. And إذا الله's Messenger had refused her, I would have accepted her.' " (Reported by AlAl-البخاري.) أبو بكر's Tender القلب: On the authority of *عائشة, may الله be pleased with her, who says: “I had seen my الوالدان practicing الإسلام بما أن I attained the age of puberty. Not a day passed but النبي ﷺ, peace and البركات be upon him, visited us, both in the morning and the evening. My الأب أبو بكر thought of building a المسجد in the courtyard of his house, and he did لذا. He used to pray and recite the القرآن الكريم in it. The polytheist women and their الأولاد used to gather around him and look at him astonishingly. أبو بكر was a softhearted man and could not help weeping بينما reciting the القرآن الكريم. The chiefs of the Quraysh disbelievers became afraid of that (ie. that their الأولاد and women might be affected by the التلاوة of القرآن الكريم)." (Reported by البخاري.) أنس, may الله be pleased with him, reports that بعد the الموت of الله's Messenger, peace and البركات be upon him, أبو بكر said to ~عمر, "Let us visit Umm Ayman as الله's Messenger, peace and البركات be upon him, used to visit her." عندما they went to her, she wept. They (أبو بكر and ~عمر) said to her, "What makes you weep? What is in store (in the next الدنيا) for الله's Messenger, peace and البركات be upon him, is better than (this worldly الحياة)." Sقال, "I weep not لأن J am ignorant of the fact that what is in store for الله's Messenger (in the next الدنيا) is better than (this worldly الحياة), but I weep لأن the Divine الوحي has ceased to come from Heaven. This moved both of them to tears and they began to weep along with her." (Reported by Moslem.) أبو بكر's Interpretation of Dreams: أبو بكر, may الله be pleased with him, was an acknowledged interpreter of dreams. النبي ﷺ, peace and البركات be upon him, himself used to consult only him for interpreting his dreams. Sa*eed Ibn Mansoor narrates on the authority of Sa* eed Ibn Al-Musayyib who said: "* عائشة, may الله be pleased with her, saw in a dream that three moons fell in her house. لذا she narrated her dream to أبو بكر - who was the best interpreter of dreams - قال: "إذا you have truly seen that, then three of the best of all الله's creation will be bur
